content.problemStatement:
Read problems statements in Russian hereChef has a special affection for sets of binary strings of equal length which have same numbers of **1's**. Given three integers **n**, **k** and **m**, your task is to find the the lexicographically  **mth** smallest string among strings  which have length n and have **k** **1's**. If no such string exists output **-1**. 

Tips:  To see what lexicographic order means . See http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lexicographical_order
constraints: 1 <= M <= number of combinations of N items taken K at a time
content.inputFormat: The first line of the input contains an integer T denoting the number of test cases. The first and only line of each test case contains three space separated integers N, K and M.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output the lexicographically mth smallest string among strings which have length n and have k 1's. If no such string exists output -1.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The set of strings in lexicographic order is "011", "101", and "110".
The set of strings in lexicographic order is "0011", "0101", "0110", "1001", "1010", and "1100".

hidden.label: maximum boundary
hidden.expectedOutput: The lexicographically smallest string with 350 bits and 175 ones.

hidden.label: edge case
hidden.expectedOutput: The lexicographically 10th smallest string with 10 bits and 5 ones.

hidden.label: general case 1
hidden.expectedOutput: The lexicographically 5th smallest string with 20 bits and 10 ones.

hidden.label: general case 2
hidden.expectedOutput: The lexicographically 10th smallest string with 30 bits and 15 ones.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use combinatorial numbers and lexicographical order. We can generate all possible binary strings of length N with K 1's and then sort them in lexicographical order. Finally, we can output the Mth smallest string.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * (N choose K) * N) - where (N choose K) is the number of combinations of N items taken K at a time
solution.spaceComplexity: O(T * (N choose K) * N) - for storing all possible binary strings
